examples.title: Generic Process Model Structure
examples.description: Demonstrates the high-level structure of a generic software process model
examples.code:
PROCEDURE GenericProcessModel
BEGIN
 WHILE project_not_complete DO
 // Framework Activities
 Communication()
 Planning()
 Modeling()
 Construction()
 Deployment()
 
 // Umbrella Activities (run throughout)
 ProjectTracking()
 RiskManagement()
 QualityAssurance()
 ConfigurationManagement()
 Measurement()
 END WHILE
END PROCEDURE
examples.explanation: This pseudocode shows that framework activities may be performed iteratively while umbrella activities run continuously throughout the project lifecycle.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is number of iterations

examples.title: Process Tailoring Algorithm
examples.description: Shows how to adapt a generic process model to specific project needs
examples.code:
PROCEDURE ProcessTailoring(projectCharacteristics, genericProcess)
BEGIN
 adaptedProcess = new Process()
 
 // Analyze project characteristics
 projectSize = projectCharacteristics.size
 teamExperience = projectCharacteristics.experience
 requirementsStability = projectCharacteristics.stability
 
 // Select and customize framework activities
 FOR each activity IN genericProcess.frameworkActivities
 IF isRelevant(activity, projectCharacteristics) THEN
 taskSet = selectTasks(activity, projectSize)
 adaptedProcess.addActivity(activity, taskSet)
 END IF
 END FOR
 
 // Customize umbrella activities
 FOR each umbrella IN genericProcess.umbrellaActivities
 intensity = determineIntensity(umbrella, teamExperience, projectSize)
 adaptedProcess.setIntensity(umbrella, intensity)
 END FOR
 
 RETURN adaptedProcess
END PROCEDURE
examples.explanation: This algorithm demonstrates how a generic process model can be tailored based on project characteristics like size, team experience, and requirements stability.
examples.timeComplexity: O(a + u) where a is activities and u is umbrella activities
examples.spaceComplexity: O(p) where p is project characteristics

examples.title: Iterative Development Pattern
examples.description: Demonstrates how iteration is implemented in software process
examples.code:
PROCEDURE IterativeDevelopment(maxIterations)
BEGIN
 iteration = 0
 currentProduct = null
 
 WHILE iteration < maxIterations AND product_not_acceptable DO
 iteration = iteration + 1
 
 // Each iteration performs all framework activities
 REQUIREMENTS = Communication()
 planIteration(REQUIREMENTS)
 model = Modeling(REQUIREMENTS)
 increment = Construction(model)
 
 // Evaluate the increment
 feedback = evaluate(increment)
 
 // Refine based on feedback (iteration)
 currentProduct = incorporateFeedback(currentProduct, feedback)
 
 // Check if product meets acceptance criteria
 IF meetsAcceptanceCriteria(currentProduct) THEN
 product_not_acceptable = FALSE
 END IF
 END WHILE
 
 Deployment(currentProduct)
END PROCEDURE
examples.explanation: This shows how modern iterative processes work - each iteration produces an increment of the product, incorporates feedback, and continues until acceptance criteria are met.
examples.timeComplexity: O(i × f) where i is iterations and f is activities per iteration
